attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-RESP-03-00203 — SEQ is provisional (derived from source AUC-003 in the 002xx batch range); maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-RESP-03 bucket.

Age NOT stated in the source note — inferred as adult (age 22) from ~75 kg body weight and the adult-range dose regimen (prednisolone 50 mg, salbutamol 5 mg neb). A parent was present, so a reviewer may reclassify to adolescent; if so, the recorded doses must be re-reviewed under the no-paediatric-dose rule (§8) and paediatric doses must NOT be synthesised.
Sex NOT stated in the source note — inferred male pending clinician confirmation; clinically neutral for this asthma presentation.
Geographic context not inferable from the source — omitted.

Telehealth reprojection applied (§6): the source is an in-person home visit (auscultation, measured vitals, pulsus paradoxus, nebulised salbutamol/ipratropium and stat oral prednisolone). Clinician-only exam/labs (auscultated wheeze, pulsus paradoxus, PEFR, ABG, precise vitals) sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; clinician-delivered nebulised/steroid therapy reprojected to recognise + escalate (000) + coach reliever-via-spacer asthma first aid. Presentation layer de-anchored to a pre-diagnosis patient/carer account.

Objective data split: patient/carer-obtainable readings (home oximeter SpO2 93%, wearable HR ~118, video-observable work of breathing, audible wheeze, empty reliever canister) placed in 01.objective_data_offered with verified:false and reliability caveats; clinician-measured vitals and exam findings sealed.

Baseline tier set to T5 (call 000): reliever failure + speech in short phrases + SpO2 93% meet national asthma first-aid criteria for calling an ambulance; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass set to T4 to avoid failing a defensible urgent self-transport answer, but under-triage below T4 is a critical failure.
Medication candidate codes captured as derived/unverified; AMT codes for ipratropium and prednisolone were NOT assigned to avoid emitting a plausible-but-wrong code — pending terminology receipt.
Dose_route_frequency values are taken verbatim from the source note / national asthma first aid — no dose was synthesised.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
